newsSince: TEL posted record FY3/26 results and guided to double-digit CY2026 growth on AI/advanced-logic and HBM stacking. Material NEW negative: the US House Select Committee report and the MATCH Act are pushing to bring foreign WFE suppliers (TEL, ASML) under US-style countrywide China bans/licensing; China was still ~40% of TEL's mix, so this directly threatens a meaningful slice of orders.

sources.label: Tokyo Electron valuation / China export controls (Yahoo)
House China hawks urge bans on chipmaking equipment (The Hill)

verdicts.rankRationale: AI-node lens: record FY results and double-digit CY26 guidance keep the AI-fab/HBM thesis intact, but the NEW US legislative escalation (House report + MATCH Act) targeting WFE-to-China is a concrete order-book risk given ~40% China mix. Net slight negative on alpha after a +98% YTD melt-up that prepays the upside.
verdicts.bull: Record sales/income, double-digit CY26 growth on AI logic + HBM stacking; broadest WFE portfolio.
verdicts.neutral: AI capex thesis intact but fully recognized; price near record highs leaves little hidden alpha.
verdicts.bear: MATCH Act / countrywide China bans threaten ~40% of mix; at +98% YTD any China hit is unhedged.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: AI/HBM capacity adds; CY26 order growth
verdicts.primaryRisk: US-led tightening of China WFE export controls

verdicts.rankRationale: CPO lens already flags TEL as 'not early or CPO-specific' with high-expectations risk. No new optical/packaging angle since Jun 9, and the fresh China export-control legislative push adds downside; the flow-driven melt-up makes it strictly more crowded. Slight negative.
verdicts.bull: Front-end breadth benefits from any AI-driven capacity build.
verdicts.neutral: No CPO-specific catalyst; relevance to this lens unchanged.
verdicts.bear: China-ban legislation plus record-high price compounds high-expectations risk with no CPO hook.
verdicts.primaryRisk: China export controls; high expectations / crowding

verdicts.rankRationale: Master-list pick-and-shovel lens: record results affirm upstream exposure, but the new US push to extend China WFE bans (House report + MATCH Act, ~40% China mix) is a genuine new downside to the bottleneck case. After a +98% YTD melt-up, alpha drifts slightly lower rather than resets.
verdicts.bull: Core upstream pick-and-shovel; record FY and double-digit CY26 guide on AI demand.
verdicts.neutral: Well-owned mega-cap; the demand thesis is fully in consensus.
verdicts.bear: Legislative China-WFE risk plus record valuation cap measured upside on the alpha lens.
